title: Symphyotrichum novi-belgii
intro: Symphyotrichum novi-belgii, also known as the New York aster, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to eastern North America, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in relieving pain, reducing fever, and protecting against infections. The bioactive constituents of Symphyotrichum novi-belgii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum novi-belgii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Symphyotrichum, which is part of the tribe Astereae. This plant is native to eastern North America and is commonly known as the New York aster. The species is further classified as a perennial herb, growing up to 2 meters in height. It produces small, purple flowers in late summer, which are a key identifier of the species.
uses: Symphyotrichum novi-belgii, commonly known as the New York aster, has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Traditionally, the New York aster has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-cancer properties and ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria. Further research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses and effectiveness of Symphyotrichum novi-belgii.

side_effects: Symphyotrichum novi-belgii, also known as the New York aster, is a flowering plant that is generally considered safe for external use.
